quoted_tweet.text:
The market reaction of punishing $META because of the increase in CapEx will turn out to be very wrong IMO.

In the $META Q325 earnings call, Zuck already explained well that if $META overbuilds their compute infrastructure for internal needs, they can sell it to external parties.

We are in one of the biggest compute demand/supply imbalances that seems to be getting worse, not better. $GOOGL GCP CEO Kurian expects demand to be bigger than supply for 10 years. 

$META's enormous AI compute capabilities will either return ROI in the form of their products (which they are already showing) or/and give great ROI by selling compute to external companies. $META investing in AI data center compute is a big asset, not a cash burn, similar to what metaverse investments were in large.

The market is viewing it as a negative, instead of an enormous strategic advantage in a compute-constrained world.

quoted_tweet.text:
Gençlerin Portföyü | Hafta Özeti 📊
Portföyümüz geçen haftanın ATH seviyesinden yaklaşık 1000$’lık düşüşle kapattı.
Haftanın açık ara 3 yıldızı şöyleydi:
•  $GOOGL %10,52
•  $AMD %7,74’lük yükselişle yeniden ATH yaptı
•  $NBIS %6,62 yükselişle onları takip etti
Haftanın hayal kırıklıkları ise $HOOD ve $META oldu. Bilançolar beğenilmedi ve hak etmedikleri satışlara maruz kaldılar. Hedefimiz bu hafta bu hisselere ekleme yapmak.
Portföyün yeni üyesi $RKLB ile yazdığımız covered call işlemi sayesinde prim toplanmış ve teminattaki hisseler cuma kapanışı ile serbest kalmıştı. Pazartesi açılışıyla birlikte agresif bir covered call daha yazarak prim toplamayı ve bu primleri düşen hisselerimize ekleme yapmak için kullanmayı planlıyoruz.
$RKLB’nin amacı diğer hisselerimizden farklı olarak; opsiyon ticaretiyle düzenli prim toplamak ve bu primleri tekrar portföye yönlendirmek.
Özetle: Portföyümüz 2 büyük düşüşü 3 güçlü yükselişle karşıladı ve haftayı çok az kayıpla kapattı.
Gelecek hafta özetiyle görüşmek üzere;
Sağlıklı, mutlu ve bol kazançlı bir hafta dilerim! 🙌✨

tweet.text:
韦德布什证券 Wedbush Securities 全球科技研究主管丹·艾夫斯（Dan Ives）最近的一次快问快答核心内容。#AI $META $NVDA $GOOGL $MSFT $MU $ORCL $TSLA 
主持人： 准备好了吗，丹？ 丹·艾夫斯： 好的，开始吧。
大科技财报季用一个词形容？ 丹：当然是看涨（Bullish）。

$META  还是微软？ 丹：微软 $MSFT 。
云端王者是微软还是谷歌？ 丹：谷歌 $GOOGL 
英伟达 $NVDA  ，是加仓还是获利了结？ 丹：加仓。
财报后最值得持有的Mag7股票？ 丹：谷歌。
目前最被低估的Mag7股票？ 丹：微软。这次抛售几乎是跳楼大甩卖。

目前最被炒作的AI股票？ 丹：我没看到特别被高估的AI股票（Pass）。
Micron还是Intel？ 丹：Micron $MU 
Oracle还是IBM？ 丹：Oracle $ORCL  ，但我仍然喜欢IBM。
Salesforce还是ServiceNow？ 丹：这里选Salesforce，但我也很喜欢ServiceNow。
一个没人讨论的中盘AI股票？ 丹：Pegasystems（佩格系统）。
如果只能投一个IPO：SpaceX、Anthropic还是OpenAI？ 丹：它们都会成功，但SpaceX会是定义类别的公司。
未来12个月最大的AI赢家？ 丹：电力（Power）。
Alphabet还有上涨空间吗？有多大？ 丹：有，还有30-40%的上涨空间。
玩AI最好的单一方式，一个名字？ 丹：英伟达（AI教父）。

谁拥有最好的AI战略？ 丹：目前是谷歌。Gemini在消费端表现突出，资本支出也在全力推进。
Palantir现在还是买点吗？ 丹：它会在未来2-3年内达到1万亿美元市值。Palantir在软件领域的地位，就像英伟达在芯片领域的地位一样。

Tesla $TSLA 什么时候能看到回报？ 丹：今年夏季到秋季，随着Robotaxi规模化，会出现转机。SpaceX的IPO对Tesla也是利好。
软件股如何应对波动？ 丹：ServiceNow被超卖、Salesforce被超卖、Workday也可以说是被超卖。Oracle在未来12-18个月有翻倍潜力。
SoundHound AI是 sleeper 还是 hype？ 丹：不是炒作，是真实策略，只是需要执行到位。我们长期看好。

tweet.text:
$AMD| Analysts forecasts are all wrong 🧵

Even UBS at $455, they modeled AMD at 
$49B FY2026
$75B FY2027
$95B FY2028

Most other analysts are at $40-$45B for FY2026. 
They are still excluding $MSFT $META @OpenAI $ORCL @LumaLabsAI $AMZN $GOOGL XAI G42 and many more. The demand for EPYC Turin and Venice are off the chart, estimated to be more than 20m units due to explosive Agentic AI demand. 

Dr. Su secured the 2nm allocation, Memory and customers, yet...

We know so far Dr. Su said Helios rack is on track to deliver in H2 2026, so 2-2.5GW is easily 
$40-$50B at conseravtive point. 

When we exclude $40-$50B, which is nonsense
$AMD will still going to do $40-$45B, could be higher if AMD could secure more EPYC Turin and Venice. 

Like I said, most analysts are still using old and false new "Delay" from SemiAnal to model AMD. 

Months ago, before @OpenAI raised $122B+, they would pound on OpenAI can't fund the first GW. Now OpenAI has roughly $170-$200B Cash, yet they still excluded OpenAI revenue out. 

On $META, it has $81.2B cash and $115.8 Operating Cash Flow, so for fuck sake, META can fund multi-GW deployment. 

AMD has the worst coverages than all other semi stocks for more than a decade. They have no idea how to understand TSMC 2nm Ramp roadmap. I will link it below. 

I understand AMD only guide quarter by quarter, but contracts are already announced, analysts don't have to be this stubborn until last minute.

“Agentic AI” (autonomous AI agents capable of multi-step reasoning, planning, and tool use) is emerging for AMD as it has the best CPUs(Turin) and EPYC Venice will widen the innovative Gap even further. Beyond traditional generative AI training/inference. Unlike pure LLM inference (GPU-heavy), agentic workloads require significant CPU orchestration for sequential decision-making, tool calling, and hybrid compute. AMD’s strong position in both EPYC server CPUs and high-memory Instinct GPUs (MI300/MI350/MI455 series) positions it well to gorw EPS(Non-GAAP) to $12-$17.

tweet.text:
The market reaction of punishing $META because of the increase in CapEx will turn out to be very wrong IMO.

In the $META Q325 earnings call, Zuck already explained well that if $META overbuilds their compute infrastructure for internal needs, they can sell it to external parties.

We are in one of the biggest compute demand/supply imbalances that seems to be getting worse, not better. $GOOGL GCP CEO Kurian expects demand to be bigger than supply for 10 years. 

$META's enormous AI compute capabilities will either return ROI in the form of their products (which they are already showing) or/and give great ROI by selling compute to external companies. $META investing in AI data center compute is a big asset, not a cash burn, similar to what metaverse investments were in large.

The market is viewing it as a negative, instead of an enormous strategic advantage in a compute-constrained world.

So we all know fundamentals will tell us what stocks to buy, and technicals can tell us when to buy...

Here's a mix of Neocloud Infrastructure updates using technicals (1D) & fundamentals:

> $CRWV. $NVDA invested $2B directly. $META signed a $21B deal through 2032. First investment grade GPU backed financing in history closed at $8.5B. 2 new BDs have appeared. Last one gave us a ~64% run.

> $NBIS. $META signed a $27B deal recently. 551% projected growth for the next 12 months... still trading at a fraction of $CRWV's valuation. $NVDA backed. Single BD has appeared. Last one gave us a ~70% run.

> $IREN. As mentioned before, it's the most de-risked IMO. Sweetwater 1 just energized. $MSFT deal live, $NVDA preferred partner. Less asymmetric but pretty hard to lose betting on it. Surprisingly, no entry confirmations... algo isn't confident yet (meaning we're still early).

> $APLD. $23B contracted backlog at a ~$9.6B MC. $CRWV anchor tenant at Polaris Forge 1. Three signed hyperscaler leases. No entry confirmations yet... same boat as $IREN.

> $SLNH. Just regained Nasdaq compliance. Renewable powered AI campus at Dorothy... Briscoe Wind Farm acquisition closes the loop. Sitting at ~$190M MC with the same tailwind as names 20x its size. Most asymmetric in the power layer. Wild pullback today, but nothing unexpected as a small cap that ran hard last week. 4H/1D/1W charts are all intact, although it dipped through 1H gold zone (where some may sell).

> $CIFR. $9.3B in long term contracts with AWS and $GOOGL. Some would argue this is a better long-term hold than $IREN. Chart looks bullish, but no entry confirmations just yet. Last BD gave us a ~48% run.

> $WULF. 73% projected growth for the next 12 months. $BTC mining pivot to AI/HPC still in early stages of being re-rated, saw this with $IREN and $CIFR. Has been outperforming $IREN and $CIFR lately. Healthy chart. Looks bullish.

I'll share more in a separate post. Plenty of asymmetric opportunities in the Neocloud theme...

Note that any early stage company will face more volatility (e.g. having to dilute to continuously build). Saw this with $PLTR early on at ~$15, and bears were piling on. We've 10x'd since then.

To clarify, it's pretty hard to go wrong betting on any of these long term. Investors aren't arguing about who's going to win, they're arguing about who's going to win more lol...
